title: Bank deposits: dollars into Infinex as USDC on Base
from: US dollars from your own bank account
to: USDC in your Infinex account on Base
split_semantics: on-off-chain: bank/fiat and wallet/crypto, now one account
deployed_facts: Infinex now supports bank deposits: send US dollars from your own bank account (ACH or wire) and they arrive in your Infinex account as USDC on Base.
Bank deposits are powered by Bridge.xyz, the stablecoin-infrastructure company acquired by Stripe.
US dollars are supported at launch, by ACH or wire.

deployed_facts.claim: EUR (SEPA) bank deposits are coming soon, not yet enabled.
deployed_facts.basis: EUR config exists in code but renders to users as status:pending ('our partner is still preparing this option'); DR-003 locks USD-only MVP
deployed_facts.scope: blog/long-form only; keep off the X post

deployed_facts: Funds settle as USDC on Base, into your main Infinex account (not vaults or imported wallets). You do not choose the destination; it is always your main Infinex wallet on Base.

deployed_facts.claim: Infinex does not add a fee on bank deposits at launch.
deployed_facts.basis: no developer fee wired in code; this may change later, never frame as a permanent promise
deployed_facts.scope: blog/long-form only; keep off the X post

deployed_facts: Verification is one-time, handled by Bridge through Persona: enter name and email, accept the terms, verify your identity. Usually a few minutes, and up to one business day.
After approval you receive reusable bank details in your name (bank name, routing number, account number) and send money the way you already do. The bank name is assigned per account, not chosen.

deployed_facts.claim: Deposits typically arrive within a day; a wire can take up to a couple of days.
deployed_facts.basis: operator's lived wire deposit landed in ~12h; settlement is rail-dependent and not guaranteed by Infinex code
deployed_facts.scope: state as expectation, not a guarantee

deployed_facts: This is deposit-only at launch. Sending dollars back out to a bank (off-ramp) is not part of this release.

deployed_facts.claim: To find it: Deposit, then Bank transfer under 'Other ways to deposit', then verify once, then USD bank transfer, then your deposit details.
Once funds are USDC on Base they can be used across Infinex: trade Hyperliquid perps, Swidge to other chains and assets, earn yield, take a position on Polymarket.
deployed_facts.scope: blog 'what's next' only, keep off the X post

deployed_facts.claim: Hyperliquid spot is coming in the weeks ahead.
deployed_facts.basis: operator roadmap, patrons informed; not bridge-grounded

feature_states.note: first time fiat lands directly on-chain inside Infinex; reader has no prior context for it
coming in weeks; forward-looking, blog only

through_action: to fold the two places the reader keeps separate, where their money sits and where it works, into one
obstacle: the reader assumes putting money on-chain means a centralized exchange, KYC dread, fees, and not knowing where the money actually went; they will skim if this reads as another fintech-rails partnership
reader_prior: fiat lives at a bank, crypto lives in a wallet, and getting between them is a chore that is usually theirs to solve
lining: on the surface, a calm practical guide to a new way to add money; underneath, the banker who spent a career inside the system that kept bank and wallet apart, now standing on the far side of that wall holding it open, certain the next doors are already coming
not_the_point: do not name the super-objective or the reader's wanting; no rails, plumbing, or feature taxonomy; no urgency or hype; do not headline the zero fee; do not say the user specifies or chooses the destination wallet; the point is not 'a new integration', it is that the wall between bank and wallet is gone
